req.title: Diagnostic report
req.statement: A clinician shall issue a DiagnosticReport referencing Observations; missing Observation links shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that a clinician can issue a DiagnosticReport referencing one or more Observations (positive path), that a report with an empty/missing Observation reference list is rejected with HTTP 422 (negative path), and that the boundary condition of exactly one Observation reference is accepted (boundary path). EP covers the well-formed request partition; NEG covers the empty-reference partition; BVA covers the minimum-valid-reference count (1). Each test uses a fresh UUID-based identifier to guarantee idempotency and determinism.

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_with_valid_observations_returns_201
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician (bearer token available via <clinician_auth_headers> fixture)
At least two Observation resources exist (IDs provided by <existing_observation_ids> fixture)
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build a DiagnosticReport payload referencing two Observation IDs
POST payload to /DiagnosticReport with clinician auth headers
expected: Response body contains a resourceType of DiagnosticReport
Response body id field is non-empty
result array in response contains two entries matching submitted Observation references
trace: R-HLT-012: report stored when valid Observation links present
EP: request belongs to the valid-input equivalence class
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
import requests

def test_diagnostic_report_with_valid_observations_returns_201(
    base_url, clinician_auth_headers, existing_observation_ids
):
    obs_id_1, obs_id_2 = existing_observation_ids[0], existing_observation_ids[1]
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "General Panel"},
        "result": [
            {"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id_1}"},
            {"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id_2}"}
        ]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "DiagnosticReport"
    assert body.get("id")
    refs = [r["reference"] for r in body.get("result", [])]
    assert f"Observation/{obs_id_1}" in refs
    assert f"Observation/{obs_id_2}" in refs

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_with_empty_observations_returns_422
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician (bearer token available via <clinician_auth_headers> fixture)
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build a DiagnosticReport payload with an empty result array
POST payload to /DiagnosticReport with clinician auth headers
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error detail indicating missing Observation references
trace: NEG: exercises the invalid/missing-reference error pathway
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
import requests

def test_diagnostic_report_with_empty_observations_returns_422(
    base_url, clinician_auth_headers
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "General Panel"},
        "result": []
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert (
        body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
        or "error" in body
        or "result" in str(body).lower()
    )

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_with_exactly_one_observation_returns_201
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician (bearer token available via <clinician_auth_headers> fixture)
Exactly one Observation resource exists (ID provided by <existing_observation_ids> fixture index 0)
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build a DiagnosticReport payload referencing exactly one Observation ID
POST payload to /DiagnosticReport with clinician auth headers
expected: result array contains exactly one entry matching the submitted Observation reference
trace: R-HLT-012: report stored when valid Observation links present
BVA: tests the minimum valid boundary of result count (N=1)
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
import requests

def test_diagnostic_report_with_exactly_one_observation_returns_201(
    base_url, clinician_auth_headers, existing_observation_ids
):
    obs_id = existing_observation_ids[0]
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "Single-Obs Panel"},
        "result": [
            {"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id}"}
        ]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "DiagnosticReport"
    result_refs = [r["reference"] for r in body.get("result", [])]
    assert len(result_refs) == 1
    assert result_refs[0] == f"Observation/{obs_id}"

import uuid
import pytest
import requests

def test_diagnostic_report_with_missing_result_key_returns_422(
    base_url, clinician_auth_headers
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "No Result Key Panel"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert (
        body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
        or "error" in body
        or "result" in str(body).lower()
    )

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_persists_and_is_retrievable_after_creation
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician (bearer token available via <clinician_auth_headers> fixture)
One Observation resource exists (ID provided by <existing_observation_ids> fixture index 0)
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: POST a valid DiagnosticReport payload referencing one Observation
Extract the assigned resource ID from the 201 response
Capture GET response status and body
expected: Retrieved resource id matches the id returned from POST
trace: R-HLT-012: report stored — state transition from pending to stored
STC: covers the created -> stored state transition for a valid report
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
import requests

def test_diagnostic_report_persists_and_is_retrievable_after_creation(
    base_url, clinician_auth_headers, existing_observation_ids
):
    obs_id = existing_observation_ids[0]
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "Persistence Check"},
        "result": [{"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id}"}]
    }
    post_resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201
    created_id = post_resp.json().get("id")
    assert created_id
    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/DiagnosticReport/{created_id}",
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    body = get_resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "DiagnosticReport"
    assert body.get("id") == created_id
